DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT LPF YERBA BUENA INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO OFFICER OF THE ASSESSOR RECORDER TO PRODUCE ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO DOCUMENT SUBPOENA. THE COURT FINDS REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE SECTION 408 TO BE AMBIGUOUS AS APPLIED TO PREVIOUS OWNERS OF THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, ESPECIALLY CORPORATE OWNERS WHICH HAVE CEASED TO HAVE ANY EXISTENCE THEREBY ELIMINATING ANY POSSIBILITY OF CONSENT. IN ACCORDANCE WITH WELL-ESTABLISHED PRINCIPLES OF LIBERAL DISCOVERY, PRACTICAL CONSTRUCTION OF STATUTES AND AVOIDANCE OF ABSURD RESULTS, THE COURT WILL ORDER PRODUCTION OF THE RECORDS IN QUESTION AND THE DESIGNATION OF SAID RECORDS AS CONFIDENTIAL UNDER THE EXISTING PROTECTIVE ORDER IN THIS CASE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 TO JUDGE PRO TEM JAMES FLEMING BY EMAIL AT JAMESFLEMING@GMAIL.COM JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMMING; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel City And County Of San Francisco Officer Of The Assessor Recorder To Produce Additional Documents In Response To Document Subpoena By Lpf Yerba Buena Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dana Andreoli of Steyer Lowenthal Alvarez & Smith LLP, Telephone (415) 421-3450, appearing for Defendant/Cross Defendant LPF Yerba Buena Inc;
Thomas Lloyd of Steyer Lowenthal Alvarez & Smith LLP, Telephone (415) 421-3400, appearing for Defendant/Cross Defendant LPF Yerba Buena Inc;
CArole F. Ruwart, Deputy City Attorney, City and County of San Francisco, Telephone (415) 554-4666, appearing for Third Party City and County of San Francisco;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LPF YERBA BUENA INC.'S Motion To Compel City And County Of San Francisco Officer Of The Assessor Recorder To Produce Additional Documents In Response To Document Subpoena.

The Court finds Revenue and Taxation Code Section 408 to be ambiguous as applied to previous owners of the subject property, especially corporate owners which have ceased to have any existence thereby eliminating any possibility of consent. In accordance with well-established principles of liberal discovery, practical construction of statutes and avoidance of absurd results, the Court will order production of the records in question and the designation of said records as confidential under the existing protective order in this case.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 to Judge Pro Tem James Fleming by email at jamesfleming@gmail.com

Judge Pro Tem: James Flemming; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
